Here is a comprehensive overview of the **US Stock Market today, Tuesday, March 10, 2026**:

---

## 📉 US Stock Market – March 10, 2026

### Major Index Performance
As of this morning, the **Nasdaq (+0.03%)** is the only major index in the green, trailed by the **Dow (-0.13%)**, **S&P 500 (-0.18%)**, and **Russell 2000 (-0.28%)**.

US equities are mostly lower on Tuesday, trimming the rebound from yesterday as pro-inflationary concerns and geopolitical uncertainty maintained investors' preference for cash. The three main equity indices hovered below the flatline.

---

### 🛢️ Key Driver: Iran Conflict & Oil Prices
A rebound in US stocks stalled as a slide in oil failed to keep boosting risk appetite, with a continuation of the war in Iran posing threats to global energy supplies.

The conflict in Iran continues today despite urgings by President Donald Trump that the days-long incursion was "very complete, pretty much."

Despite a continuation of conflict in the Middle East, **U.S. Crude Oil is 6.83% lower at $92.20**, thanks to talk about the release of strategic petroleum reserves as important shipping corridors remain shut.

---

### 📊 Dow Jones Movers
The **Dow Jones (-0.49%)** is struggling, with just eight stocks in the 30-strong index advancing. Gains in **Cisco Systems (+2.81%)** and **Caterpillar (+1.56%)** are not enough to make up for steep losses from **Salesforce (-3.61%)** and **UnitedHealth Group (-1.68%)**, among others.

---

### 🏅 Safe Havens & Bonds
Safe havens like **gold (+2.27% to $5,219.60)** and **silver (+5.52%)** are running higher this morning, while the **Dollar Index (-0.52% to 98.66)** is lower. The **10-year Treasury yield** is 1.7 basis points lower at **4.117%**.

---

### 🏢 Earnings & Economic Data to Watch
Investors are primarily focused on the **February Consumer Price Index (CPI) report**, due for release tomorrow, Wednesday, March 11. This report is expected to provide definitive clues on whether inflation is truly "sticky" or if the Federal Reserve can maintain its current pause on interest rates.

**Oracle** is slated to report earnings today, along with **Franco-Nevada Corp**, **BioNTech**, and **AeroVironment**, among others.

---

### 💡 Tech & Notable Stocks
Following a sharp recovery in equities on hopes the conflict may be nearing an end, the S&P 500 edged lower. While tech shares outperformed, traders are bracing for **Oracle Corp.'s earnings** later Tuesday, looking for clues on its spending plans amid rising skepticism around artificial intelligence.

Meanwhile, **Amazon** inched higher after kicking off the sale of its **$37 billion bond offering**, and **Nvidia** and **AMD** were steady after TSMC reported a **30% jump in sales** for the first two months of the year.

---

**Summary:** Markets remain volatile and largely under pressure on March 10, 2026, driven by the ongoing US-Iran conflict, elevated oil prices, and uncertainty around inflation. Investors are closely watching oil developments, the upcoming CPI report, and Oracle's earnings for direction.

content.text:
## US Stock Market Today — March 10, 2026

Markets are mostly **lower** today, with geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns weighing on sentiment.

---

### Major Indices (as of this morning)
| Index | Change |
|---|---|
| **Nasdaq** | +0.03% (only green) |
| **Dow Jones** | -0.49% |
| **S&P 500** | -0.18% |
| **Russell 2000** | -0.28% |

---

### Key Drivers

- **Iran Conflict** — The ongoing US-Iran conflict is the dominant market driver. Oil is down ~6.8% to $92.20/barrel after Trump signaled the incursion was "pretty much complete," but uncertainty remains.
- **Safe Havens Rising** — Gold is up +2.27% to $5,219/oz, Silver +5.52%, as investors seek protection.
- **Dollar Weakening** — Dollar Index -0.52% to 98.66.

---

### Stocks to Watch Today
- **Oracle** — Reporting earnings today; traders watching AI spending plans closely.
- **Amazon** — Slightly higher after launching a $37B bond offering.
- **Nvidia / AMD** — Steady after TSMC reported +30% sales jump.
- **Salesforce** — Down -3.61%, dragging the Dow.

---

### What's Next
Tomorrow's **February CPI report** (Wed, March 11) is the big upcoming catalyst — it could signal whether the Fed needs to adjust its rate pause.

---

Sources:
- [The Street — Stock Market Today Mar. 10, 2026](https://www.thestreet.com/latest-news/stock-market-today-march-10-2026)
- [24/7 Wall St. — S&P 500 Down Even as Oil Sinks](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/03/10/stock-market-live-march-10-2026-sp-500-spy-down-even-as-oil-sinks/)
- [Bloomberg — Markets Wrap Mar. 10](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-09/oil-drops-as-trump-signals-iran-conflict-near-end-markets-wrap)
- [Charles Schwab — Stock Market Update](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open)
